skill.name: context-save
skill.description: Creates a concise session handoff note when the user asks to switch windows, then saves it under docs/context-sessions/.
skill.summary: Save current work context as a structured handoff note for later recovery.
skill.author: Claudate

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and entropy findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, readable Chinese prose, and example text. The confirmed risk is that the skill directs agents to persist session summaries into repository files, which can accidentally retain secrets or proprietary context.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Context Persistence
medium_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to summarize current session context, completed work, pending work, key files, and technical notes, then save that content under docs/context-sessions/. This is useful for handoffs, but it can persist secrets, proprietary requirements, credentials, or sensitive file paths if redaction is not performed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs the agent to collect session details and save them to a repository directory. I found no evidence of network exfiltration or code execution, so the risk is local persistence and later accidental disclosure.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline-code markers, fenced examples, and placeholder paths. No executable Ruby, shell command, subprocess call, or command interpolation is present in the skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visibly Markdown formatting and examples, not runnable source code. The skill contains no script file or command execution API.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding at the frontmatter description is not supported by the file content. The line describes saving Markdown session notes and does not define or use a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain metadata text about a documentation directory. I did not find evidence of hashing, encryption, or cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to example notes about a media identifier in a sample project task. It does not instruct the agent to inspect the host system, enumerate processes, or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is part of a user-facing example output, not a command or reconnaissance workflow. No evidence of host discovery behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: The high entropy finding appears to be caused by multilingual prose, Markdown symbols, and emoji in a readable Markdown file. No binary blob, encrypted payload, encoded script, or obfuscated content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is human-readable Markdown from the first lines onward. Entropy alone is a weak signal here, and semantic review found no hidden payload.

content.value_statement: Long AI coding sessions can lose important decisions when work moves to a new window. This skill creates a structured handoff note with completed work, pending tasks, key files, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Detects a specific user request to save context before switching windows.
Summarizes the current task, completed work, pending work, and next actions.
Formats the handoff as a Markdown session document.
Stores the handoff under docs/context-sessions/ using a timestamped filename.
Reports the saved path and the number of unfinished tasks.
limitations: It depends on the agent accurately summarizing the active conversation.
It does not automatically redact secrets or private business context.
It does not restore context by itself; a separate resume workflow is expected.
It can create repository files that may be committed unless reviewed.

use_cases.title: Developer Window Handoff
use_cases.description: Save implementation progress before moving a long coding task to a fresh AI window.

use_cases.title: Team Continuity Notes
use_cases.description: Create a clear task summary that another teammate can review before continuing work.

use_cases.title: Project Session Archive
use_cases.description: Keep lightweight records of active project decisions, open tasks, and important files.

prompt_templates.title: Save Basic Context
prompt_templates.prompt: 换窗口处理- Please save the current session context with completed tasks, pending tasks, key files, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Save With Priority Review
prompt_templates.prompt: 换窗口处理- Save the current context and group unfinished tasks by high, medium, and low priority.

prompt_templates.title: Save Technical Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: 换窗口处理- Save the current context, including important technical decisions, known problems, and files the next window should inspect first.

prompt_templates.title: Save Redacted Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: 换窗口处理- Save a context handoff, but redact secrets, tokens, private customer data, and credentials before writing the session file.

output_examples.input: Save the current session before I open a new window.
output_examples.output: A Markdown handoff note is created with the task summary, completed work, pending work, key files, cautions, and recommended next action.

output_examples.input: Prepare context for another developer to continue this feature.
output_examples.output: The saved note explains the current feature state, lists unfinished tasks by priority, and identifies the files that matter most.

output_examples.input: Create a redacted session summary for later recovery.
output_examples.output: The handoff note keeps the project state and next steps while excluding credentials, tokens, and private customer details.

best_practices: Review the generated note before committing it to a shared repository.
Ask for redaction when the conversation includes credentials or private data.
Use specific task names so session files are easy to find later.
anti_patterns: Do not save raw secrets, tokens, credentials, or customer data.
Do not treat the saved handoff as a verified project specification.
Do not rely on this skill as the only record for critical decisions.

faq.question: What trigger does this skill use?
faq.answer: It is designed to run when the user sends the Chinese trigger phrase for switching windows.

faq.question: Where does it save the handoff?
faq.answer: It saves a Markdown session file under docs/context-sessions/.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No evidence of shell execution was found. The command findings are Markdown formatting false positives.

faq.question: Can it leak sensitive information?
faq.answer: It can persist sensitive conversation details locally if the agent includes them in the note.

faq.question: Does it restore the saved context?
faq.answer: No. It prepares a saved note for a separate context resume workflow.

faq.question: Should teams publish saved sessions?
faq.answer: Teams should review and redact saved sessions before sharing or committing them.
